attended_events.title: Sputnik Open Screening
attended_events.description: A recurring independent short-film screening and filmmaker gathering at Sputnik Kino.
attended_events.why_selected: This is the strongest explicit positive taste example: free, social, independent, short-film focused, and surprising.

venue.name: Sputnik Kino
venue.why_watched: Independent cinema and the recurring Open Screening, retained as the strongest attended and loved taste example.

source_evidence.evidence: Retained only as attended and loved taste evidence; this file is never included in the live Explore list.

events.title: The Dark Rooms: VEINS
events.description: Ten international light and sound artists occupy the hidden corridors and chambers of an old cinema with audiovisual installations and machines.
events.why_selected: It is a short-run, site-specific light and sound exhibition with many Berlin premieres.

occurrence.title: The Dark Rooms: VEINS
occurrence.accessibility_notes: 18+. Timed personalized ticket and ID required. The building has several floors and many stairs; wheelchair users are asked to contact the organizer in advance. The public checkout total starts at EUR 35.20 including fees.

venue.name: The Dark Rooms
venue.why_watched: Short-run immersive exhibitions built around light, sound, darkness, unusual architecture, and international media artists.

events.source_name: The Dark Rooms and Love Your Artist

source_evidence.source_name: The Dark Rooms and Love Your Artist
source_evidence.evidence: The official ticket page lists 19 July, 10:00 to 23:00, with tickets still offered; the exact venue remains officially undisclosed.

events.title: Lina Lapelytė: Werkgespräch
events.description: The talk examines the artistic process behind We Make Years Out of Hours, including time, voice, and collective experience.
events.why_selected: It gives process context for a participatory installation structured through voice and time.

occurrence.title: Lina Lapelytė: Werkgespräch
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The talk is free with a valid museum admission ticket and requires no registration.

venue.name: Hamburger Bahnhof
venue.why_watched: Contemporary art, performance, and the unusually good free Berlin Beats garden series.

source_evidence.evidence: The official listing gives 19 July, 12:00 to 13:00, no registration, and no extra charge beyond a valid museum ticket.

events.title: What Time Is [Your] Color? - Bosphorus: A Field of Becoming
events.description: Faruk Bil and computational artist Calin Segal turn nearly 10,000 photographs of the Bosphorus surface into an interactive installation across P61's displays, with music by O.Bee.
events.why_selected: It combines computational image-making, interaction, and sound at a watched digital-art venue.

occurrence.title: What Time Is [Your] Color? - Bosphorus: A Field of Becoming
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The ticket page says tickets are almost gone but still offers booking. Detailed accessibility information is unknown.

venue.name: P61 Gallery
venue.why_watched: Digital and time-based art shown across 4K displays and LED architecture, including computational art, animation, video, and sound.

source_evidence.evidence: The official exhibition and ticket pages list this program from 12:00 to 14:00, with EUR 14 regular and EUR 11 reduced admission and active booking.

events.title: Lina Lapelytė: Workshoptalk
events.description: The English-language talk focuses on visitor participation and the Historic Hall's role in the installation's choreography.
events.why_selected: It connects participatory sound work with the architecture that shapes its performance.

occurrence.title: Lina Lapelytė: Workshoptalk
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The talk is free with a valid museum admission ticket and requires no registration.

venue.name: Hamburger Bahnhof
venue.why_watched: Contemporary art, performance, and the unusually good free Berlin Beats garden series.

source_evidence.evidence: The official listing gives 19 July, 13:00 to 14:00, no registration, and no extra charge beyond a valid museum ticket.

events.title: Nude with Skeleton
events.description: A live re-performance of Marina Abramović's 2002 work places a living body with a skeleton inside Balkan Erotic Epic.
events.why_selected: It is a daily durational performance embedded in a watched venue's exhibition.

occurrence.title: Nude with Skeleton
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The performance has no extra charge but requires an exhibition ticket. Recommended for ages 16 and over; timed exhibition entry is recommended and current slot availability is unknown.

venue.name: Gropius Bau
venue.why_watched: Contemporary art, performance, moving image, sound, and public programs with strong international and experimental work.

source_evidence.evidence: The official exhibition page lists Nude with Skeleton Wednesday through Monday from 14:00 to 18:00, with EUR 15 regular and EUR 10 reduced exhibition admission.

events.title: P61 Daytime: Digital Dimensions and Breathing with Chaos
events.description: Two digital exhibitions combine animation, video, rhythm, and sound across P61's display architecture.
events.why_selected: The paired program centers audiovisual techniques at a watched digital-art venue.

occurrence.title: P61 Daytime: Digital Dimensions and Breathing with Chaos
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The ticket page says tickets are almost gone but still offers booking. Detailed accessibility information is unknown.

venue.name: P61 Gallery
venue.why_watched: Digital and time-based art shown across 4K displays and LED architecture, including computational art, animation, video, and sound.

source_evidence.evidence: The official ticket page lists Digital Dimensions and Breathing with Chaos from 14:00 to 19:00, with EUR 14 regular and EUR 11 reduced admission and active booking.

events.title: Bjørn Melhus: THE END OF THE BEGINNING
events.description: Bjørn Melhus introduces the exhibition's film program and explains the selected works and source material behind it.
events.why_selected: It directly addresses the moving-image and sampled-source methods used in the installation.

occurrence.title: Bjørn Melhus: THE END OF THE BEGINNING

venue.name: silent green
venue.why_watched: Concerts, sound work, film, performance, and large installations across the Kuppelhalle, Betonhalle, studios, and lawn.

source_evidence.evidence: The official page lists the free artist talk at 19:00 on 19 July in the Kuppelhalle.

events.title: Error Music
events.description: Participants make, build, and code electronic music with women producers while exploring the museum's sound environment.
events.why_selected: It is a practical creative-coding and music-production workshop, with its eligibility limits stated explicitly.

occurrence.title: Error Music
occurrence.accessibility_notes: Four-day workshop from 21 to 24 July for ages 12–17 who identify as girls, trans, intersex, or non-binary; capacity is limited. Times after the first 11:00–15:00 session are not stated on the listing.

venue.name: Hamburger Bahnhof
venue.why_watched: Contemporary art, performance, and the unusually good free Berlin Beats garden series.

source_evidence.evidence: The official listing gives a free, limited-capacity four-day workshop from 21 to 24 July, with the first session from 11:00 to 15:00.

events.title: American Movie Night: Elvis
events.description: The first of three free music-film evenings at the Neulich beer garden screens Elvis in English with German subtitles under the open sky.
events.why_selected: It is a free, low-commitment outdoor film night with a music focus and no booking friction.

occurrence.title: American Movie Night: Elvis
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The film is shown in English with German subtitles and no registration is required.

venue.name: Neulich at THF
venue.why_watched: An outdoor beer garden that occasionally hosts free screenings and unusual public programs.

source_evidence.evidence: The official page lists Elvis on 21 July at 19:00, outdoors, free, without registration, in English with German subtitles.

events.title: silent green Open Lab #7
events.description: Shu and Frieda turn a 1990s Cantonese ballad into unstable sound research about migration and memory; GLTR Mauer follows with music shaped by shoegaze, game soundtracks, hardcore, hyperpop, and house.
events.why_selected: It is free, artist-led, and close to the operator’s positive taste for independent, social work that can still surprise.

venue.name: silent green
venue.why_watched: Concerts, sound work, film, performance, and large installations across the Kuppelhalle, Betonhalle, studios, and lawn.

source_evidence.evidence: The official page lists free admission, doors at 19:00, and a 20:00 start on 21 July in the Kuppelhalle.

events.title: B-Movie: Lust & Sound in West-Berlin 1979–1989
events.description: Jörg A. Hoppe's documentary uses interviews and archive footage to follow Mark Reeder through West Berlin's music, avant-garde, and squatter scenes.
events.why_selected: It is a music-centered independent screening at a favorite cinema.

occurrence.title: B-Movie: Lust & Sound in West-Berlin 1979–1989
occurrence.accessibility_notes: 92 minutes, age 16+, original version with English subtitles. This screening is in the Kinobar; online purchases add a 10% service fee.

venue.name: Sputnik Kino
venue.why_watched: Independent cinema and the recurring Open Screening, retained as the strongest attended and loved taste example.

source_evidence.evidence: The official programme lists the 92-minute music documentary at 21:30 on 21 July in the Kinobar with an active ticket link; Kinobar admission is EUR 7.50.

events.title: American Movie Night: A Complete Unknown
events.description: The second free music-film evening at the Neulich beer garden screens A Complete Unknown in English with German subtitles.
events.why_selected: It extends the open-air series without requiring a ticket, while remaining distinct enough to choose instead of the first night.

occurrence.title: American Movie Night: A Complete Unknown
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The film is shown in English with German subtitles and no registration is required.

venue.name: Neulich at THF
venue.why_watched: An outdoor beer garden that occasionally hosts free screenings and unusual public programs.

source_evidence.evidence: The official page lists A Complete Unknown on 22 July at 19:00, outdoors, free, without registration, in English with German subtitles.

events.title: Major Tom: völlig losgelöst
events.description: Peter Schilling's music is presented through 360-degree fulldome visuals and a 3D sound design built for the planetarium.
events.why_selected: The production is a concrete example of synchronized large-format image and spatial sound.

occurrence.title: Major Tom: völlig losgelöst
occurrence.accessibility_notes: Recommended from age 12. No language comprehension is required. Wheelchair tickets are available only through the venue ticket hotline.

venue.name: Zeiss-Großplanetarium
venue.why_watched: Fulldome audiovisual programs with large-scale projection and spatial sound.

source_evidence.evidence: The official page lists 22 July at 21:30, 55 minutes, EUR 16 regular and EUR 12 reduced, with online tickets offered.

events.title: Date Night Special: Discover Brancusi
events.description: Two people can visit the Brancusi exhibition during a dedicated two-hour evening slot at the Neue Nationalgalerie.
events.why_selected: It is a finite after-hours format at a watched museum, with the two-person ticket terms clearly stated.

occurrence.title: Date Night Special: Discover Brancusi
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The EUR 32 booking includes two exhibition tickets and is available online only.

venue.name: Neue Nationalgalerie
venue.why_watched: Modern art plus occasional performance, music, film, architecture, and after-hours programs.

source_evidence.evidence: The official listing gives 23 July from 18:00 to 20:00, EUR 32 for two tickets, with online registration available.

events.title: American Movie Night: Walk the Line
events.description: The final free music-film evening at the Neulich beer garden screens Walk the Line in English with German subtitles.
events.why_selected: It is a public open-air music film with no ticket or registration barrier.

occurrence.title: American Movie Night: Walk the Line
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The film is shown in English with German subtitles, no registration is required, and the THF Tower site is listed as fully accessible.

venue.name: Neulich at THF
venue.why_watched: An outdoor beer garden that occasionally hosts free screenings and unusual public programs.

source_evidence.evidence: The official page lists Walk the Line on 23 July at 19:00, outdoors, free, without registration, in English with German subtitles.

events.title: Berlin Beats: MARRØN
events.description: MARRØN plays a rhythm-driven Afro-deep techno set in the Hamburger Bahnhof garden.
events.why_selected: It is a free, finite open-air electronic-music performance at a watched venue.

occurrence.title: Berlin Beats: MARRØN
occurrence.accessibility_notes: No registration is required. The outdoor event is free; museum exhibitions require a valid ticket and remain open until 20:00.

venue.name: Hamburger Bahnhof
venue.why_watched: Contemporary art, performance, and the unusually good free Berlin Beats garden series.

source_evidence.evidence: The official listing gives 23 July from 19:00 to 22:00 in the Hamburger Bahnhof garden, with free admission and no registration.

events.title: Durchlüften: Peki Momés, Yalla Miku, and afrodisiac
events.description: Peki Momés moves between Turkish disco and city pop, Yalla Miku fuse Horn of Africa traditions with experimental trance, and afrodisiac closes with a DJ set.
events.why_selected: The evening brings traditional sources into direct contact with electronics, trance, and club music.
events.organizer: Stiftung Humboldt Forum im Berliner Schloss

occurrence.title: Durchlüften: Peki Momés, Yalla Miku, and afrodisiac
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The Schlüterhof and toilets are step-free; seating is limited and admission may pause if the courtyard reaches capacity. Loud music and strobe lighting may be used.

venue.name: Humboldt Forum
venue.why_watched: Free and unusual international concerts, sound, performance, film, talks, and public programs, including the Durchlüften summer festival.

source_evidence.evidence: The official festival page lists Peki Momés at 19:00, Yalla Miku at 20:30, and DJ afrodisiac at 22:00 on 23 July, with free admission.

events.title: Oran Ray: The Drummer
events.description: Oran Ray builds jungle and breakbeat live from drums, hardware electronics, bass, and evolving synth textures on the THF Tower roof.
events.why_selected: The set centers real-time electronic performance rather than a conventional DJ format.

occurrence.title: Oran Ray: The Drummer
occurrence.accessibility_notes: The THF Tower is listed as fully accessible; the evening box office and bar accept card payment only.

venue.name: THF Tower
venue.why_watched: Rooftop music, sound work, readings, film, and participatory summer programs.

source_evidence.evidence: The official page lists doors at 18:00, a 19:00 start, a 22:00 finish, EUR 5 regular and EUR 3 reduced admission, and a live set combining drums with hardware electronics.

events.title: Maresz, Del Gesù, and The Midnight Audience
events.description: Maresz combines slowcore, electronics, and steady beats; Del Gesù performs a live audiovisual set; The Midnight Audience builds image-less sound environments from tension and residue.
events.why_selected: The bill includes a specifically identified live A/V set and spatial, soundtrack-oriented sound work.
events.organizer: silent green and Music From Beyond

occurrence.title: Maresz, Del Gesù, and The Midnight Audience

venue.why_watched: Concerts, sound work, film, performance, and large installations across the Kuppelhalle, Betonhalle, studios, and lawn.
